Use this agent when a task is complex enough that upfront planning will save time and reduce rework during implementation.

features.name: general-purpose Agent
features.summary_ja: 汎用エージェント
features.description_ja: 特化していない汎用エージェント。複雑な調査やマルチステップタスクに使える。
features.summary_en: A versatile general-purpose sub-agent for complex research and multi-step tasks spanning both reading and writing operations.
features.description_en: A non-specialized general-purpose agent capable of handling a wide variety of tasks including both read and write operations. Use this when the task does not fit cleanly into the Explore or Plan agent roles, or when you need a sub-agent that can both investigate and implement as part of the same delegated task.

features.name: Custom Subagents
features.summary_ja: 独自サブエージェントの作成
features.description_ja: .claude/agents/<name>.md にエージェント定義を置くと、自分専用のサブエージェントが作れる。
features.summary_en: Create your own custom sub-agents by placing a Markdown definition file at .claude/agents/<name>.md with instructions and tool access.
features.description_en: Create project-specific sub-agents by placing a markdown definition file at .claude/agents/<name>.md in the project directory. The file defines the agent's system prompt, allowed tools, and behavioral constraints. Custom agents are then available for delegation via the Task tool, just like built-in agent types.

features.name: Worktree Isolation
features.summary_ja: git worktreeでエージェント隔離
features.description_ja: サブエージェントを別のgit worktreeで動かしてmainと隔離。並列開発の事故防止に。
features.summary_en: Run sub-agents in separate git worktrees to completely isolate their filesystem changes from the main working tree.
features.description_en: Runs sub-agents in dedicated git worktrees, completely isolating their filesystem changes from the main working tree. This prevents accidental conflicts when multiple agents are modifying related files in parallel. Use this pattern for parallel feature development or when running experiments that should not affect the main working directory until explicitly merged.

features.name: Skills System
features.summary_ja: 再利用可能なスキルシステム
features.description_ja: .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md でスキル定義。description のキーワードでトリガーされ、Claudeが自動的に呼び出す。
features.summary_en: Reusable instruction sets defined in SKILL.md files that Claude automatically invokes when user input matches their trigger keywords.
features.description_en: Skills are reusable instruction sets defined in .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md files within the project directory. Each skill has a description field that acts as a trigger — when user input matches the description's keywords, Claude automatically invokes the skill. Skills can define the specific tools they need and include detailed step-by-step procedures.

features.name: SKILL.md Structure
features.summary_ja: スキル定義ファイルの構造
features.description_ja: frontmatter に description（トリガー文）、name、tools などを書く。本文は手順・ルール・テンプレート。
features.summary_en: A SKILL.md file starts with YAML frontmatter containing name, description, and tools, followed by a procedure instruction body.
features.description_en: A SKILL.md file contains YAML frontmatter at the top with key fields: name (the skill's identifier), description (the trigger keywords that activate automatic invocation), and tools (the list of tools the skill is allowed to use). The body of the file contains the skill's instructions, step-by-step procedures, rules, and output templates that Claude follows when executing the skill.

features.name: Plugin Marketplace
features.summary_ja: 公式プラグインマーケット
features.description_ja: Anthropic公式や有志によるプラグイン（スキル/エージェントのバンドル）を配布・取得できるマーケット。
features.summary_en: The official Plugin Marketplace for browsing and installing skill-and-agent plugin bundles from Anthropic and community contributors.
features.description_en: A curated marketplace for distributing and obtaining plugins — reusable bundles of skills and sub-agents — published by Anthropic and community contributors. Browse available plugins, read their descriptions, and install them with /plugin. The marketplace provides a centralized discovery point for extending Claude Code's capabilities beyond the built-in features.

features.name: Skill Triggers
features.summary_ja: スキルの自動起動トリガー
features.description_ja: SKILL.md の description に書いたキーワードが入力にマッチすると Claude が自動でスキルを呼び出す仕組み。

examples.title: Description example
examples.code: description: PRレビューしたい時に使う

features.summary_en: Claude automatically invokes a skill when user input contains trigger keywords defined in the skill's description frontmatter field.
features.description_en: The automatic invocation mechanism that makes skills activate without explicit commands. When the user submits a prompt, Claude checks whether any installed skill's description field matches the prompt keywords. If a match is found, Claude invokes the skill automatically and follows its defined procedure. Write clear, specific description text to ensure skills trigger only when appropriate.

features.name: MCP Overview
features.summary_ja: Model Context Protocol
features.description_ja: 外部ツール・APIをClaudeに統合するためのオープンプロトコル。GitHub、Slack、DBなどへの橋渡し。
features.summary_en: Model Context Protocol is an open standard that connects Claude to external tools and APIs like GitHub, Slack, and databases.
features.description_en: An open protocol for integrating external tools, APIs, and data sources directly into Claude's context. MCP servers act as bridges between Claude and external services such as GitHub, Slack, databases, and custom internal APIs. Once configured in .mcp.json, the tools provided by MCP servers are available to Claude in every session automatically.

features.name: MCP stdio Server
features.summary_ja: 標準入出力ベースのMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: プロセス起動して stdin/stdout でやりとりするMCPサーバー。最も一般的。Pythonやnpx で簡単に動かせる。
features.summary_en: The most common MCP server type, which communicates with Claude via stdin/stdout through a locally spawned child process.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates with Claude via standard input and standard output by spawning a child process locally. This is the most common and widely supported transport type. MCP stdio servers can be written in Python, Node.js, or any language with an MCP SDK, and are typically started with npx, uvx, or a direct executable path.

features.name: MCP SSE Server
features.summary_ja: Server-Sent Events型のMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: HTTP経由でストリーミング通信するMCPサーバー。クラウドホスト型サービスに向く。
features.summary_en: An HTTP-based MCP server type using Server-Sent Events streaming to communicate with Claude, suited for cloud-hosted remote services.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates over HTTP using the Server-Sent Events protocol to stream responses back to Claude. Well-suited for cloud-hosted services or remote APIs that need to push streaming data. Configure the server URL in .mcp.json and Claude will connect to it at session start.

features.name: MCP HTTP Server
features.summary_ja: REST/HTTP型のMCPサーバー
features.description_ja: 標準的なHTTPでやり取りするMCPサーバー。シンプルなリクエスト/レスポンスモデル。
features.summary_en: A standard REST/HTTP MCP server type using simple request-response communication, well-suited for straightforward API integrations.
features.description_en: An MCP server that communicates over standard HTTP using a simple request-response model without streaming. Suitable for straightforward API integrations where streaming is not required. Easier to implement and debug than SSE-based servers for services that return complete responses.

features.name: Status Line
features.summary_ja: プロンプト下部のカスタム表示
features.description_ja: git ブランチ、モデル名、コンテキスト残量など、自分の好みに合わせて統合バーを表示できる。
features.summary_en: A customizable status bar displayed below the prompt that shows git branch, active model, context usage, and other metrics.
features.description_en: A customizable integration bar displayed just below the prompt input field. Can show a variety of real-time metrics including the current git branch, active model name, context window usage percentage, and other session information. Configure the status line content through a skill or by editing settings.json.

features.name: Keybindings
features.summary_ja: キーボードショートカット
features.description_ja: ~/.claude/keybindings.json でキー割当をカスタマイズ。chord（複数キー）対応。
features.summary_en: Customize Claude Code keyboard shortcuts in ~/.claude/keybindings.json with support for both single keys and multi-key chord sequences.
features.description_en: Customize key assignments for Claude Code actions by editing the ~/.claude/keybindings.json configuration file. Supports both single-key bindings and chord sequences (multi-key shortcuts pressed in sequence). Use /keybindings to open a guided walkthrough of the configuration format and see which actions can be bound to custom keys.

features.name: VS Code Extension
features.summary_ja: VS Code拡張機能
features.description_ja: VS CodeにClaude Codeを統合。エディタ内でClaudeと対話、現在のファイル/選択範囲を即文脈化。
features.summary_en: Integrates Claude Code into VS Code so the current file and code selection are automatically added to Claude's context.
features.description_en: Integrates Claude Code directly into Visual Studio Code as an extension. Interact with Claude from within the editor using the integrated panel. The currently open file and any selected code are automatically included in Claude's context, making it easy to ask questions or request edits on the code you are actively working with.

features.name: JetBrains Plugin
features.summary_ja: JetBrains IDE プラグイン
features.description_ja: IntelliJ / WebStorm / PyCharm などへClaude Codeを統合。VS Code版と同等。
features.summary_en: Integrates Claude Code into IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, and other JetBrains IDEs with full feature parity to the VS Code extension.
features.description_en: Integrates Claude Code into the JetBrains IDE family including IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, PyCharm, and others. Provides feature parity with the VS Code extension, including automatic context from the active file and code selection. Install from the JetBrains Plugin Marketplace and configure with your Claude Code credentials.

features.name: Fast Mode
features.summary_ja: 高速応答モード
features.description_ja: /fast コマンドで Opus 4.6 ベースの高速応答に切替。Opus 4.6 でのみ利用可能。
features.summary_en: Fast Mode uses Claude Opus 4.6 for lower-latency quicker responses; toggle it on or off at any time with the /fast command.
features.description_en: Enables a faster response mode specifically for Claude Opus 4.6, reducing response latency at the cost of some depth. Fast Mode is only available when using the Opus 4.6 model. Toggle it on or off at any time during a session with the /fast command. Useful when you need rapid back-and-forth iteration and are willing to trade depth for speed.
